$note: Captured snapshot of the COMPARED forecast/intelligence product for the azimuth benchmark (synthesis/benchmark.py). Quoted by product + capture date; NOT surfaced as an azimuth channel and NOT an L1 source. Refresh with pull_benchmark_foils.py.

foil.title: Inflation and rates pressure from Iraq infrastructure fragility state
foil.scenario_short: Inflation and rates pressure in Iraq is probable (59%) over the next 30 days, stemming from infrastructure fragility (44% pressure, 67% state confirmation) and energy supply shock, with 2 urgent critical signals...
foil.perspective_strategic: The 59% probability of inflation and rates pressure in Iraq, driven by infrastructure fragility (44% pressure, 67% state confirmation) and energy supply shock, indicates a notable economic vulnerability linked to internal stability.
foil.perspective_contrarian: The lack of more directly matched reporting on Iraq and the 75% confidence level suggest that the inflation and rates pressure could be mitigated if new, stabilizing signals emerge, preventing a decisive break towards shock.
foil.actor_lens: Commodity traders: Price whether stress in Iraq becomes durable over the 30d. Rebalance positions if the probability path moves away from 59%.
foil.top_signal: Iraq infrastructure fragility state is feeding inflation and rates pressure through energy supply shock, with 44% pressure and 67% state confirmation

seismic-infrastructure.absence_reason: No product publishes a seismic forecast — earthquakes are not predictable, so there is no forecast/intel alternative to azimuth's observed USGS record. This is the honest inverse: for an observed natural-hazard fact, the sourced record IS the only trustworthy source.
